description: VAR was introduced in major football leagues starting with the 2018 World Cup and 2019-20 Premier League season. Initial support (~55%) eroded to ~45% as controversial implementations, long delays, and marginal offside calls frustrated fans. Opinion inverted from majority support to majority opposition.

key_events.description: 2018 World Cup: VAR widely praised for correcting clear errors. France-Australia penalty correctly awarded. High-profile saves of justice. Support peaks at ~58%.
Premier League 2019-20: first months of VAR bring controversies. Goals disallowed for millimeter offsides. Celebrations killed by long reviews. Fan frustration mounts.
Liverpool's Firmino goal ruled out by armpit offside line. 'Armpit offside' becomes meme. Atmosphere at grounds suffers — fans don't know whether to celebrate goals.
Multiple Premier League clubs' fan groups protest VAR with banners. Handball rule changes create more confusion. 'Clear and obvious error' standard criticized as inconsistent.
IFAB and Premier League announce VAR reforms: thicker offside lines, faster reviews, pitchside monitor usage encouraged. Slight recovery in support.

calibration_notes: Technology adoption backlash pattern — initial optimism followed by implementation-driven disillusionment. BBC/YouGov polls tracked support declining from ~55-60% (World Cup 2018) to low 40s during 2019-20 Premier League. Partial recovery with reforms. Tests: technology vs. tradition tension, implementation quality mattering more than concept, emotional experience (celebration uncertainty) driving opinion more than rational accuracy gains.
